questions.question: What is the key difference in the `type` field between an element VNode and a component VNode?
options: Element VNodes use `null`; component VNodes use a string
Element VNodes use a string like `'div'`; component VNodes use the component definition object
Element VNodes use a number ID; component VNodes use a Symbol
Both use strings, but element VNodes are lowercase and component VNodes are PascalCase

questions.question: Does a component appear as a tag in the actual browser DOM?
options: Yes, Vue adds a custom element with the component name as the tag
Yes, but only if `inheritAttrs` is set to true
No, only the component's rendered output (element VNodes) appears in the DOM
No, unless the component uses `<Teleport>` to mount itself

questions.question: Compared to elements, what makes component VNodes more expensive?
options: Components must always fetch data from an API before rendering
Components require a reactive instance, `setup()` execution, and their own VNode tree
Components block the main thread while the virtual DOM diffing runs
Components cannot be reused, so Vue creates a new class for each occurrence
